title: EEE-Bench 开放题
rewritten_question_text: Two long, thin straight wires \( A \) and \( B \) carry currents \( I_A = 1 \, \text{A} \) and \( I_B = 2 \, \text{A} \), respectively, with the current directions and positions as shown in the image. Let \( B_A \) and \( B_B \) be the magnitudes of the magnetic induction at point \( P \) produced by \( I_A \) and \( I_B \), respectively. The ratio of \( B_A \) to \( B_B \) is \( \) (Answer with a number).

rewritten_question_text: For the closed loop system shown in the figure, where \(k>0\) and \(\alpha>0\), what is the steady-state error for a ramp input \(R(s)=\frac{\alpha}{s^2}\)?

title: EEE-Bench 子题 1
rewritten_question_text:
The circuit shown in the image is used to provide regulated voltage (5 V) across the 1 kΩ resistor. Assume that the Zener diode has a constant reverse breakdown voltage for a current range, starting from a minimum required Zener current, \(I_{Z_{min}} = 2 \text{ mA}\) to its maximum allowable current. The input voltage \(V_I\) may vary by 5% from its nominal value of 6 V. The resistance of the diode in the breakdown region is negligible.

What is the value of \(R\)?

title: EEE-Bench 子题 2
rewritten_question_text:
The circuit shown in the image is used to provide regulated voltage (5 V) across the 1 kΩ resistor. Assume that the Zener diode has a constant reverse breakdown voltage for a current range, starting from a minimum required Zener current, \(I_{Z_{min}} = 2 \text{ mA}\) to its maximum allowable current. The input voltage \(V_I\) may vary by 5% from its nominal value of 6 V. The resistance of the diode in the breakdown region is negligible.

What is the minimum required power dissipation rating of the Zener diode?

title: EEE-Bench 子题 1
rewritten_question_text: In the circuit shown in the figure, the current source \(I = 1\text{ A}\), the voltage source \(V = 5\text{ V}\), \(R_1 = R_2 = R_3 = 1\Omega\), \(L_1 = L_2 = L_3 = 1\text{ H}\), \(C_1 = C_2 = 1\text{ F}\). What is the current (in A) through \(R_3\)?

title: EEE-Bench 子题 2
rewritten_question_text: In the circuit shown in the figure, the current source \(I = 1\text{ A}\), the voltage source \(V = 5\text{ V}\), \(R_1 = R_2 = R_3 = 1\Omega\), \(L_1 = L_2 = L_3 = 1\text{ H}\), \(C_1 = C_2 = 1\text{ F}\). What is the current (in A) through the voltage source \(V\)?

title: EEE-Bench 开放题
rewritten_question_text:
The silicon sample with unit cross-sectional area shown in the figure is in thermal equilibrium. The following information is given: \( T = 300 \, K \), electronic charge \( q = 1.6 \times 10^{-19} \, C \), thermal voltage = 26 mV, and electron mobility = \( 1350 \, cm^2/V\text{-}s \).

The magnitude of the electron drift current density at \( x = 0.5 \, \mu m \) is ______.

The value of \(R_2\) (in \(\Omega\)) for which \(I_2 = 100\ \mu\text{A}\) is ________.
